message:
fix(core,runtime,widgets): harden numeric edge cases across animation, budget, and widget subsystems

Guard against division-by-zero, NaN propagation, and integer overflow
in several hot paths:

Animation (ftui-core):
- Fade::raw_progress and Slide::progress now return 1.0 immediately
  when duration is zero, preventing 0.0/0.0 division that yields NaN.
- Timeline::progress applies the same zero-duration guard before
  computing current_time / total_duration.

Runtime (ftui-runtime):
- AllocationBudget::observe early-returns None when the observation
  value is NaN, preventing poisoned statistics from triggering false
  budget alerts.

Widgets (ftui-widgets):
- CommandPalette::adjust_scroll replaces bare addition/subtraction with
  saturating_add/saturating_sub to prevent usize overflow when
  scroll_offset + max_visible wraps around.
- VirtualizedList scroll-into-view logic uses saturating_add for the
  same overflow-safety reason.
- ProgressBar::ratio explicitly maps NaN to 0.0 before clamping,
  since f64::NAN.clamp() returns NaN in Rust and can propagate
  downstream to floor() and rendering math.
- Sparkline::lerp_color applies the same NaN-to-zero guard before
  clamping the interpolation parameter t.
- HeightPredictor prediction interval uses the (n+1) quantile formula
  instead of bare n, correcting off-by-one in the upper confidence
  bound calculation.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
